rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE HOM FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Response To Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Petitioner's Motion to Compel is granted in part, no timely opposition having been filed. Respondents' late-filed opposition is not considered. The responding parties are ordered to respond to Special Interrogatories 1 through 8 by producing information and records as to all accounts they hold jointly or individually or as successors in interest to Lester Hom, limited to cash deposits into those accounts between June 2010 and April 2014 within seven days after a cash withdrawal from the Wells Fargo joint accounts held by Robert, Marie, Brian, and Anna Hom ("the joint accounts") if such deposits within any such seven-day period are equal to or greater than $300. Responding parties must also produce information and records of any deposits into any of their accounts by check or transfer from those joint accounts during the June 2010 through April 2014 time period. The responding parties must also produce information and records responsive to Special Interrogatories 10 through 14 with deposit information for the specified checks. All information produced may be produced subject to the provisions of the proposed Protective Order attached as Exhibit C to the Yee Declaration filed in connection with this motion, which the Court now adopts. The responding parties may redact all but the last four digits of any account number, and they may redact names of any non-party joint account holder. The responding parties are further ordered to produce documents responsive to Request for Production No. 26 subject to the same limits and procedures set forth above. Sanctions are denied because responding parties could reasonably contend that the requests as framed were overbroad and unduly invasive. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, February 20, 2018.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE D J H DATED JULY 24, 1990 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoena For Personal Appearance And Production Of Business Records Against Third Party Witness Albert Cromedy
rulings.Rulings: The Motion to Compel is granted in part, no timely opposition having been filed. Mr. Cromedy is ordered to produce any text messages in his possession or control to or from Raymond Apodaca during the period October 1, 2015 through September 15, 2017 concerning Mr. Pongracz or Mr. Pongracz's estate planning to the extent such texts are stored on his cell phones and were not lost, damaged, altered, or overwritten as the result of the routine, good faith operation of Mr. Cromedy's cell phone service provider. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $915. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, February 20, 2018.
